pages.full_text:
Copies (of Niaux Cave paintings) in [?Prehisto], Salon Noir [?bisons]. www.sesta.fr Taraskon sur Ariège

much enjoyed. It was good to have the drawings I made clearer through infra red camera removing 15,000 years encrustations I had not realised before that they used natural curves in rock face too. An astounding
photo_captions: Copies (of Niaux Cave paintings) in [?Prehisto], Salon Noir [?bisons]. www.sesta.fr Taraskon sur Ariège
pages.notes: Page contains a large glued-in photo of cave painting reproductions. The vertical caption beside/on the photo is partially obscured — '[?Prehisto]' may be 'Préhistoire' or similar; '[?bisons]' is a reasonable read but partly cut off. The text below the photo continues mid-sentence from a previous page. No handwritten page number visible in top-left (photo occupies that region). www.sesta.fr appears to be a website reference for the Niaux cave paintings. 'Taraskon sur Ariège' could alternatively be 'Tarascon-sur-Ariège'.

pages.full_text:
reproduction of 5, 7+13 year olds! footprints in sand of cave. Carvings of reindeer in bone. An English commentary through head phones. We're off to buy bread. We bought myrtle pie (blackcurrant or mulberry?) + drove on to le Malageou Camping *** near Ax-les-Thermes. There was a terrific thunder storm with heavy rain in the night. Very warm.
__Tuesday August 10th 2004__
We are camped next to a river which has space set aside for fishermen in wheelchairs. Crowded campsite but quiet + clean. The woman used a computer to give us a printed out map showing [?] pitches still unused "(" + we could choose our own + then go back + tell her the number. A local market at the campsite - I bought honey (10 k. away, rhododendron, so not local!) + goats cheeses - she had photos of her goats. Last night John beat Eve at scrabble with Clippers over a red - 83 points.
pages.notes: Page number 30 visible at top right. The left margin of this page shows text running vertically ('www.sesta.fr Taraskon sur Ariège') which is the continuation/overflow of the caption from the left page — not transcribed again here. 'le Malageou' is the spelling as written; could also be 'le Malageou' or similar — uncertain. 'Clippers' in the scrabble reference may be the word played. The word '13' before 'year olds' may read '12' — best reading is 13. The symbol '[?]' replaces a symbol used to denote pitches on the printed map.
